query: 2026 Cuban crisis: current state of US-Cuba negotiations and tensions as of July 2026. What high-level US official visits to Cuba have occurred or are planned in summer 2026?

key_facts: Cuban FM Bruno Rodríguez stated on June 30, 2026 that US-Cuba negotiations show 'no progress,' describing US conduct as 'generally respectful' but with 'constant threats' and 'coercive measures.'
High-level US visits to Cuba occurred in April-May 2026, NOT summer 2026; no high-level US visits are publicly scheduled for summer 2026 as talks are at a standstill.

April 10, 2026: State Dept delegation led by Jeremy Lewin (foreign assistance director) and Michael Kozak (Latin America diplomat) met Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro ('Raúlito') in Havana — first US government plane to land since Obama's 2016 visit.
May 14, 2026: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met Cuban officials including Rodríguez Castro and Interior Minister Lázaro Álvarez Casas.
February 2026: Secretary of State Marco Rubio secretly met 'Raúlito' at a CARICOM summit in St. Kitts (back-channel).

US demands: release of political prisoners, increased political freedoms, economic/governance reforms, compensation for confiscated assets; incentives offered include Starlink, $100M humanitarian aid, agricultural support (conditional).
Cuba's top priority demand: lifting the US oil/energy blockade, which caused the country to run out of oil and diesel (announced May 14, 2026).
A UN General Assembly debate/vote on the US embargo is scheduled for July 7, 2026; Cuba accuses the US State Department of pressuring UN members to block it.
US began blocking oil tankers to Cuba in February 2026 (targeting Pemex and others), enforced via tariffs on countries providing petroleum (EO signed Jan 30, 2026).

May 1, 2026 Executive Order established a new Cuba-related sanctions program; June 2026 sanctions targeted President Díaz-Canel and family; May 21, 2026 indictment issued against Raúl Castro for the 1996 Brothers to the Rescue shootdown.
Reports of a US ~two-week deadline for Cuba to release political prisoners; Cuba denies any 'ultimatum' was issued.
Havana continues to experience widespread nightly blackouts amid the energy crisis; despite the impasse, Cuba says it will 'remain open to dialogue' while rejecting political change.
